title: Sweet Creamy Malaysian Fish Pilaf
description: A light and flavorful Malaysian dish featuring poached fish, pickle juice, and a creamy sauce. Perfect for a low-fat lunch or dinner.

ingredients.name: White fish fillet (e.g., cod or tilapia)

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the rice and stir to coat with oil and mix with onion mixture. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked.
While the rice is cooking, poach the fish fillets in a separate saucepan with enough water to cover them. Cook until opaque and flake easily with a fork, about 8-10 minutes.
In a separate pan, combine coconut milk, pickle juice, sugar, and lime juice. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook until the sauce has thickened slightly, about 5 minutes.
Stir in salt and black pepper to taste.
To serve, place a portion of the cooked rice on a plate, top with a poached fish fillet, and spoon the creamy sauce over the top.
